query:
UPDATE `safetyTopics` SET `content` = 'Key Points:
• OSHA requires fall protection at 6 feet in construction (29 CFR 1926.501). This is non-negotiable — every worker at height must be protected.
• Before starting any elevated work, inspect your harness for frayed webbing, damaged buckles, or worn stitching. Check lanyards for cuts, kinks, or chemical damage. Verify anchor points can hold 5,000 lbs per worker.
• Know the three systems: Fall Arrest (stops a fall in progress — harness + lanyard + anchor), Fall Restraint (prevents reaching the edge), and Positioning (holds you in place for hands-free work, like on rebar).
• Never tie off to conduit, vent pipes, or temporary structures. Use engineered anchor points only.
• After any fall event, the harness MUST be removed from service and inspected by a competent person. Even if it looks fine, internal fibers may be damaged.
• Real-world example: A framer in Utah fell 14 feet when his lanyard wasn''t connected to an anchor point — he was wearing the harness but hadn''t clipped in. Always verify the connection.

Discussion Questions:
1. What fall hazards exist on our current job site today?
2. When was the last time you inspected your harness?
3. Can everyone identify the anchor points on this job?

Remember: Falls are the #1 killer in construction. Take 30 seconds to clip in — it could save your life.' WHERE `id` = 1;
